findings.message: write-good.Passive (warning): 'is supposed' may be passive voice. Use active voice if you can.
write-good.E-Prime (suggestion): Try to avoid using 'is'.
write-good.TooWordy (warning): 'It is' is too wordy.
write-good.TooWordy (warning): 'it is' is too wordy.

`updated` is earlier than `date`.
Body contains an `# h1`. The page template emits the h1 from `title`; body headings start at `h2`.
findings.excerpt: # This heading should not be here

findings.message: Image `../../assets/a11y-bot-form.png` has empty alt text. Alt text conveys the information in the image, not the filename.
`description` is 165 chars — over the ~155-char SERP limit. Add a short `seoDescription` override.
`title` is 89 chars — over the ~60-char SERP limit. Add a short `seoTitle` override.
No `tags`. Tags feed the writing index and topical grouping.
Image `/images/from-public.png` is not a relative `src/assets/` reference. Images in `public/` get no optimisation and no intrinsic dimensions, which costs CLS.
mechanical fix: The updated date (2026-07-01) is earlier than the published date (2026-07-18), which is logically impossible for an 'updated' timestamp.
answer first: The post opens with an h1 inside the body (the title is already declared in frontmatter), and there are no h2/h3 sections at all, so no section opens with its conclusion.
findings.excerpt: # This heading should not be here
findings.evidence: The entire post is a single undifferentiated block with no section structure, making the answer-first requirement impossible to satisfy.

findings.message: buried lede: The post has no discernible lede at all — there is no opening statement of what the post is about or what the reader should take away.
findings.excerpt:
A refacrtor of the review pipeline was the damanging part; the accessibiltiy work
was fine.
findings.evidence: The only body text is a single sentence fragment that presupposes context never established in the post, so the actual point (if any) is entirely absent.

findings.message: self containment: This sentence is unintelligible in isolation: 'the review pipeline,' 'the damaging part,' and 'the accessibility work' all refer to antecedents that are never introduced anywhere in the post.
findings.excerpt:
A refacrtor of the review pipeline was the damanging part; the accessibiltiy work
was fine.
findings.evidence: An AI answer engine extracting this section would have no basis for understanding which pipeline, which refactor, or which accessibility work is being discussed.

findings.message: unsupported: The claim that the refactor was 'the damaging part' is stated as fact with no evidence, measurement, or source provided anywhere in the post.
findings.excerpt:
A refacrtor of the review pipeline was the damanging part; the accessibiltiy work
was fine.
findings.evidence: No data, incident report, or prior section establishes what damage occurred or how it was attributed to the refactor rather than to other causes.

findings.message: Suggested edit was too large to apply automatically (changes 6 tokens (limit 3)) and is recorded as a suggestion only. The first image has an empty alt attribute, which is a factual defect (missing required content) distinct from style.
findings.evidence: The model proposed replacing this with: "![A description of the a11y bot form](../../assets/a11y-bot-form.png)". Editorial patches are limited to mechanical-class edits (<= 3 tokens, <= 20 characters) — anything larger is a prose rewrite, which the Steward never applies automatically.
